content: Francis Ford Coppola directed "The Godfather," which was released in 1972 and is widely considered one of the greatest films ever made. The movie, based on Mario Puzo's novel, starred Marlon Brando as Vito Corleone and Al Pacino as his son Michael.
raw_content: Francis Ford Coppola directed "The Godfather," which was released in 1972 and is widely considered one of the greatest films ever made. The movie, based on Mario Puzo's novel, starred Marlon Brando as Vito Corleone and Al Pacino as his son Michael.
